{"output":"Executed batch 2 task T2 in `AgentChat.tsx`. I extracted `sendNow(item)` so optimistic send metadata is snapped at actual send time, added the required id-matched optimistic clear in the error path, and reworked `handleSend(rawText?)` to snapshot attachments into a `QueuedMessage`, enqueue while the send path is busy, and only send immediately when idle. Checkpoint written to `reigh-app/.megaplan/plans/add-a-message-queue-to-the-20260414-1343/execution_batch_2.json`. Verification: `npx eslint src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.tsx` passes; `npm test -- AgentChat` still fails on the known pre-existing Router issue in `AgentChat.test.tsx` (`useLocation()` outside a Router).","files_changed":["src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.tsx",".megaplan/plans/add-a-message-queue-to-the-20260414-1343/execution_batch_2.json"],"commands_run":["sed -n '130,470p' src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.tsx","git status --short src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.tsx .megaplan/plans/add-a-message-queue-to-the-20260414-1343/execution_batch_1.json","rg -n \"queue|sendNow|handleSend|optimisticMessage|sendingRef|isProcessing|sendMessage\\.isPending\" src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.tsx","git diff -- src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.tsx","npx eslint src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.tsx","npm test -- AgentChat","nl -ba src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.tsx | sed -n '155,470p'","nl -ba src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.tsx | sed -n '470,500p'"],"deviations":["`npm test -- AgentChat` remains red for the same pre-existing baseline issue from batch 1: `AgentChat.test.tsx` renders `AgentChat` without a Router, so `useLocation()` throws before assertions. I did not scope-creep into test harness changes in this batch."],"task_updates":[{"task_id":"T2","status":"done","executor_notes":"Extracted `sendNow(item)` in `src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.tsx` and moved optimistic-message setup into that callback so `priorTurnCount` and `sentAtMs` are snapped at send time. `sendNow` now clears `optimisticMessage` by id-match in its catch path and always releases `sendingRef` in finally. Reworked `handleSend(rawText?)` to snapshot attachments from the current clip selection into a `QueuedMessage`, clear the draft only for typed sends, enqueue while busy (`sendingRef`, `isProcessing`, `sendMessage.isPending`, `optimisticMessage`, or existing queue), and otherwise call `sendNow` immediately. Verification: `npx eslint src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.tsx` passes. `npm test -- AgentChat` still fails on the pre-existing Router issue in `AgentChat.test.tsx` where `useLocation()` is called outside a Router.","files_changed":["src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.tsx"],"commands_run":["npx eslint src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.tsx","npm test -- AgentChat"]}],"sense_check_acknowledgments":[{"sense_check_id":"SC2","executor_note":"Yes. `sendNow` now captures `priorTurnCount` and `sentAtMs` at call time, clears `optimisticMessage` only when the ids match in catch, and always releases `sendingRef` in finally. `handleSend` now snapshots attachments from the current clips, queues whenever the send path is busy, and sends immediately only when idle."}]}

Implementing batch 3 in `AgentChat.tsx`. I’m adding the queue-drain effect on top of the existing `sendNow` path, keeping dequeue-after-success semantics intact, then I’ll re-run file lint and the full `AgentChat` module.

item.text: The drain behavior matches the batch requirement now. I’m checkpointing the T3 result and SC3 acknowledgment, then I’ll return the structured JSON only.

{"output":"Executed batch 3 task T3 in `AgentChat.tsx`. I added the queue-drain `useEffect` so queued messages only auto-send when the chat is idle, the head item is awaited before any dequeue happens, and failed sends intentionally remain at the front of the queue. Checkpoint written to `reigh-app/.megaplan/plans/add-a-message-queue-to-the-20260414-1343/execution_batch_3.json`. Verification: `npx eslint src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.tsx` passes; `npm test -- AgentChat` still fails on the known pre-existing Router issue in `AgentChat.test.tsx` (`useLocation()` outside a Router).","files_changed":["src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.tsx",".megaplan/plans/add-a-message-queue-to-the-20260414-1343/execution_batch_3.json"],"commands_run":["sed -n '395,520p' src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.tsx","git status --short src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.tsx .megaplan/plans/add-a-message-queue-to-the-20260414-1343/execution_batch_2.json","rg -n \"sendNow|setQueue|queue.length|optimisticMessage|sendingRef\" src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.tsx","npx eslint src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.tsx","npm test -- AgentChat","nl -ba src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.tsx | sed -n '440,525p'"],"deviations":["`npm test -- AgentChat` remains red for the same pre-existing baseline issue from earlier batches: `AgentChat.test.tsx` renders `AgentChat` without a Router, so `useLocation()` throws before assertions. I did not scope-creep into test harness changes in this batch."],"task_updates":[{"task_id":"T3","status":"done","executor_notes":"Added the queue-drain `useEffect` in `src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.tsx`. It now exits while a send is in flight, the agent is processing, the mutation is pending, an optimistic message is still active, the queue is empty, or there is no active session/timeline. When idle, it reads `queue[0]`, awaits `sendNow(next)`, and only then removes the head with a `prev[0]?.id === next.id ? prev.slice(1) : prev` guard so failed sends remain visible and reorder races do not drop the wrong item. Verification: `npx eslint src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.tsx` passes. `npm test -- AgentChat` still fails on the pre-existing Router issue in `AgentChat.test.tsx` where `useLocation()` is called outside a Router.","files_changed":["src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.tsx"],"commands_run":["npx eslint src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.tsx","npm test -- AgentChat"]}],"sense_check_acknowledgments":[{"sense_check_id":"SC3","executor_note":"Yes. The drain effect awaits `sendNow(next)` before slicing the queue, uses `prev[0]?.id === next.id` when removing the head, leaves failed items at the front by catching without slicing, and will naturally re-fire once `optimisticMessage` clears because that state is in the dependency list and the early-return guard."}]}

Implementing batch 4 in `AgentChat.tsx`. I’m adding the queued-message stack UI plus local reorder/delete handlers directly above the attachment strip, then I’ll run file lint and the full `AgentChat` module again.

item.text: The queue cards render where they need to now. I’m writing the batch 4 checkpoint with the T4 update and SC4 acknowledgment, then I’ll return the structured JSON only.

{"output":"Executed batch 4 task T4 in `AgentChat.tsx`. I added the queued-message stack above the clip attachment strip, wired local up/down/delete handlers, and rendered each queued item with line-clamped text, an attachment count badge, and boundary-disabled reorder controls. Checkpoint written to `reigh-app/.megaplan/plans/add-a-message-queue-to-the-20260414-1343/execution_batch_4.json`. Verification: `npx eslint src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.tsx` passes; `npm test -- AgentChat` still fails on the known pre-existing Router issue in `AgentChat.test.tsx` (`useLocation()` outside a Router).","files_changed":["src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.tsx",".megaplan/plans/add-a-message-queue-to-the-20260414-1343/execution_batch_4.json"],"commands_run":["sed -n '1,220p' src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.tsx","sed -n '600,760p' src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.tsx","git status --short src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.tsx .megaplan/plans/add-a-message-queue-to-the-20260414-1343/execution_batch_3.json","npx eslint src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.tsx","npm test -- AgentChat","nl -ba src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.tsx | sed -n '280,360p'","nl -ba src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.tsx | sed -n '700,790p'"],"deviations":["`npm test -- AgentChat` remains red for the same pre-existing baseline issue from earlier batches: `AgentChat.test.tsx` renders `AgentChat` without a Router, so `useLocation()` throws before assertions. I did not scope-creep into test harness changes in this batch."],"task_updates":[{"task_id":"T4","status":"done","executor_notes":"Rendered the queued-message stack in `src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.tsx` immediately above the clip attachment strip and added local reorder/delete handlers. Each queued row now shows line-clamped text, a `📎 N` attachment badge when attachments exist, disabled up/down controls at the boundaries, and an `X` remove control. Styling uses design tokens (`bg-muted/50`, `bg-background/80`, existing button variants) with no hardcoded colors. Verification: `npx eslint src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.tsx` passes. `npm test -- AgentChat` still fails on the pre-existing Router issue in `AgentChat.test.tsx` where `useLocation()` is called outside a Router.","files_changed":["src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.tsx"],"commands_run":["npx eslint src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.tsx","npm test -- AgentChat"]}],"sense_check_acknowledgments":[{"sense_check_id":"SC4","executor_note":"Yes. Queued cards now render above the clips strip with line-clamped text, a `📎 N` badge for attachments, disabled `ChevronUp`/`ChevronDown` boundary controls, and an `X` delete button, all styled with design tokens rather than hardcoded colors."}]}

Implementing batch 5 in `AgentChat.tsx`. I’m loosening the input/send disabled gates so typing and submitting can continue while the current turn is processing, and I’ll make the placeholder explicitly switch to queue mode when the chat is busy.

item.text: The remaining failure is concrete now: a failed queued head re-enters the drain path automatically. I’m fixing that in `AgentChat.tsx` first, then I’ll rerun the full module, add a one-off verification script for the failed-head scenario, and finish with repo-wide lint/typecheck.

The minimal fix is a blocked-head sentinel keyed by queued message id. A failed head stays in place and stops the drain while it remains at `queue[0]`; removing it or switching sessions clears that block and lets the queue continue.
The pause-on-failure guard is in place. I’m rerunning lint on the touched files and the full `AgentChat` module now; if that clears, I’ll do the scratch verification and finish with repo-wide checks.
